column1: APS SYSTEM NOTE
column9: SPU uses a MODIFIED and UNIQUE APS system that differs from most SA universities. Base points: 8 for 90–100%, 7 for 80–89%, 6 for 70–79%, 5 for 60–69%, 4 for 50–59%, 3 for 40–49%, 2 for 30–39%, 1 for 0–29%. BONUS POINTS (added on top of base): +2 for Mathematics (pure) at 60%+ (Level 5+); +1 for Mathematics at 50–59% (Level 4) or 40–49% (Level 3). LANGUAGE BONUS: +2 for any official language taken as Home Language (HL) at Level 5+; +1 at Level 4 or Level 3. LIFE ORIENTATION EARNS POINTS: 4 pts for 90–100%, 3 for 80–89%, 2 for 70–79%, 1 for 60–69%, 0 for below 60%. This means the SPU maximum APS is considerably higher than the standard 42-point maximum. KEY: The APS minimums listed per programme reflect the SPU-calculated score (with bonuses included), not a standard 7-point scale total. Single campus: Kimberley, Northern Cape. Applications via CAO. LOLT (Language of Learning and Teaching) is English — all programmes require English HL L4 or FAL L5.
